article_title: Esports World Cup 2026 Moves to Paris as Organizers Cite Regional Instability in Historic First International Relocation
summary: Clean submission — all claims verified against sources, quotes verbatim, no corrections needed. Allowlist warning is a configuration gap, not a content issue.

content_preview.title: Esports World Cup 2026 Moves to Paris as Organizers Cite Regional Instability in Historic First International Relocation
content_preview.summary: The Esports World Cup will leave Saudi Arabia for the first time, relocating to Paris from July 6 to August 23 with its $75 million prize pool intact.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Esports World Cup will be held outside Saudi Arabia for the first time in the event's history, with the Esports Foundation announcing on May 20 that the 2026 edition will take place in Paris, France, from July 6 through August 23. The relocation preserves the full competitive structure of the tournament — 2,000+ players from 200 clubs across 100+ countries, competing across 24 games in 25 tournaments for a prize pool exceeding $75 million — but marks a significant shift for an e...

recommendations: Add esportsworldcup.com, gosugamers.net, and liquipedia.net to config/source_allowlist.txt — all are primary or established esports sources used in a verified article.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 692-word News article with clear Overview, contextual sections, and an Analysis section. Appropriate use of headers and sourced inline links. No editorializing or loaded language. Neutral throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources read from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (esportsworldcup.com official press release) — confirms all key facts: Paris dates July 6–August 23, 2,000+ players, 200 clubs, 100+ countries, 24 games, 25 tournaments, $75M prize pool, 750M viewers, 350M hours, 8M peak concurrent, 97 broadcast partners, 28 platforms, 35 languages. Reichert quotes confirmed verbatim. source-1.html.gz (prnewswire.com) — confirms the announcement date May 20, Reichert's full quote, meeting with Macron at Palais Élysée on May 19 (per image caption). source-2.html.gz (gosugamers.net) — confirms Macron's full quote via his X account: 'The EWC will be held in Paris! This is a first that honors us. Thank you to Saudi Arabia for its trust. We are ready to organize this 2026 e-sports World Cup. Very proud to welcome the world again.' Article quotes selectively but accurately (no misrepresentation). source-3.html.gz (liquipedia.net via Archive.org, captured April 29 2026 — predates Paris announcement, still shows Riyadh, expected). Confirms $30M Club Championship, $45M across tournaments, $7M top prize, schedule dates. Note: Liquipedia archive snapshot does not contain the 15% increase figure — that claim is sourced via internal link to prior MH article, not attributed to Liquipedia directly.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific facts verified against cited sources. Prize pool breakdown ($30M Club + $45M tournaments), schedule dates, audience stats, and Macron/Reichert quotes all confirmed verbatim. The three domains not on the allowlist (esportsworldcup.com, gosugamers.net, liquipedia.net) are legitimate and reputable primary/specialist esports sources — the allowlist warning is a configuration gap, not an editorial concern.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual throughout. Analysis section is measured and evidence-grounded — no speculation beyond what the facts support. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. The March 2026 article covered the prize pool announcement; this covers the Paris relocation, a distinct and significant development. The April 2026 Esports Nations Cup article is a separate event.
recommendations: Add esportsworldcup.com, gosugamers.net, and liquipedia.net to config/source_allowlist.txt for future esports submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. All claims are source-backed, all direct quotes are verbatim, structure is clean, tone is neutral. Approve without corrections.
